pages.title: Bathroom Creation
pages.content_raw:
Bathroom Creation

In need of More Bathrooms?

Unwanted box room, Cupboard under the stairs, ensuite from unused closet space. We can create a new bathroom wherever required. 

Trading space used for one purpose to become a bathroom always has a consequence, there's always an argument for not doing it.

Bathrooms are used on a daily basis and creation of bathroom space could remove a huge amount of the stress associated with there not being enough of them.

Excellent work by professionals who respect your home. Liam turned up when he said he would, cleaned up at the end of each day and did a brilliant job. He gave us good advice and nothing was too much trouble. I would highly recommend him. Carole Firth Customer

pages.title: Bathroom Emergency Repairs & Maintenance
pages.content_raw:
Bathroom Emergency Repairs & Maintenance

General Maintenance can actually avoid the need for most repairs. Not only is this a preventative measure for future more costly repairs, it often reduces utility consumption costs and it's good practice in general.  It stops the daily grind of that tap that needs a Vulcan death grip to turn it off, the shower head that fires everywhere but down, the plug that acts more like a strainer toilet that's on a constant flush. It illogical to think that a leak will fix itself. The discolouration in your ceiling is caused by plaster/paint being saturated by water. This is either damp, a roof leak or a pipe leak - the first step is to identify the cause - let us help. Over time, when building materials are saturated with liquid (especially if they dry then become resaturated), they start to deteriorate, the damage will spread and the problem severity and cost to rectify escalates. There is never a "good time" to spend money on unbudgeted costs, but there is a "good amount" to be spent - as little as possible (on the condition that it's a full resolution). Excellent work by professionals who respect your home. Liam turned up when he said he would, cleaned up at the end of each day and did a brilliant job. He gave us good advice and nothing was too much trouble. I would highly recommend him. Carole Firth Customer

pages.title: Privacy Policy
pages.content_raw:
Who we are

Our website address is: http://lvbathrooms.co.uk.

What personal data we collect and why we collect it

Comments

When visitors leave comments on the site we collect the data shown in the comments form, and also the visitor’s IP address and browser user agent string to help spam detection.

An anonymized string created from your email address (also called a hash) may be provided to the Gravatar service to see if you are using it. The Gravatar service privacy policy is available here: https://automattic.com/privacy/. After approval of your comment, your profile picture is visible to the public in the context of your comment.

Media

If you upload images to the website, you should avoid uploading images with embedded location data (EXIF GPS) included. Visitors to the website can download and extract any location data from images on the website.

Contact forms

Cookies

If you leave a comment on our site you may opt-in to saving your name, email address and website in cookies. These are for your convenience so that you do not have to fill in your details again when you leave another comment. These cookies will last for one year.

If you visit our login page, we will set a temporary cookie to determine if your browser accepts cookies. This cookie contains no personal data and is discarded when you close your browser.

When you log in, we will also set up several cookies to save your login information and your screen display choices. Login cookies last for two days, and screen options cookies last for a year. If you select "Remember Me", your login will persist for two weeks. If you log out of your account, the login cookies will be removed.

If you edit or publish an article, an additional cookie will be saved in your browser. This cookie includes no personal data and simply indicates the post ID of the article you just edited. It expires after 1 day.

Embedded content from other websites

Articles on this site may include embedded content (e.g. videos, images, articles, etc.). Embedded content from other websites behaves in the exact same way as if the visitor has visited the other website.

These websites may collect data about you, use cookies, embed additional third-party tracking, and monitor your interaction with that embedded content, including tracking your interaction with the embedded content if you have an account and are logged in to that website.

Analytics

Who we share your data with

How long we retain your data

If you leave a comment, the comment and its metadata are retained indefinitely. This is so we can recognize and approve any follow-up comments automatically instead of holding them in a moderation queue.

For users that register on our website (if any), we also store the personal information they provide in their user profile. All users can see, edit, or delete their personal information at any time (except they cannot change their username). Website administrators can also see and edit that information.

What rights you have over your data

If you have an account on this site, or have left comments, you can request to receive an exported file of the personal data we hold about you, including any data you have provided to us. You can also request that we erase any personal data we hold about you. This does not include any data we are obliged to keep for administrative, legal, or security purposes.

Where we send your data

Visitor comments may be checked through an automated spam detection service.

Your contact information

Additional information

How we protect your data

What data breach procedures we have in place

What third parties we receive data from

What automated decision making and/or profiling we do with user data

Industry regulatory disclosure requirements

pages.title: Bathroom Refurbishment
pages.content_raw:
Bathroom Refurbishment

In need of Modernisation? With over 1,000 tile sizes, textures and colours to choose from, your choice of options have come a long way from the days when you'd have a handful to select from.  Fittings commonly chosen for your kitchen sink or a public bathroom aren't in our selection, we will reluctantly fit them but we'll do everything we can to provide a better option. We love being proud of our work, as our customer, your satisfaction with the final product is as critical to us as being paid. Modernisation refurbishment doesn't need to cost a fortune it can be as simple as a re-grout or do enough to make it feel like a completely new space! How smart is your bathroom?  LED lights are pretty much standard with every new installation. Yes you can save money on electricity consumption and bulb replacement but there's a lot more to discuss! Kelvin doesn't work for us! it's the exact measure of yellow in your bulb. We're not checking WhatsApp, we want to know that the wifi is within range for dimming and RGB control. When we ask about your bathroom routine we're not judging, we'e setting up automation. We install mirrors that are backlit, with a weather display, USB charging port whilst hiding a Sonos surround sound system. Steam room showers that change colour with temperature, Toilets that are a bit to intimate! We are scratching the surface with our technological statements, this is your bathroom and the possibilities are endless.  Excellent work by professionals who respect your home. Liam turned up when he said he would, cleaned up at the end of each day and did a brilliant job. He gave us good advice and nothing was too much trouble. I would highly recommend him. Carole Firth Customer

pages.title: Home
pages.content_raw:
We make beautiful
Bathrooms. Wet Rooms. Powder Rooms. Toilets. Lavatories. Water Closets. Privy's.

Why choose us?

Don't believe the reviews, don't believe the referral that brought you here.

Call us, meet us and understand why we are the go-to contact for most local businesses that demand a higher standard in terms of quality and durability.

We're proud to be the best and not the biggest!

Our Services

Quality and value combined - we uphold our promises regarding quality and timescales

Filling this space with beautiful tiles and fancy bathroom furniture would certainly catch your eye, but it detracts from our message... 

We can supply and install anything that you'e seen whilst perusing instagram, pintrest or any other social media platform. 

We can steer you away from inferior products, source premium fixtures and fittings at trade prices. Unlike other suppliers, we don't push endorsed products that have a huge mark-up. 

Honest, flexible and experienced. 

We do offer free impartial advice on projects that we are not involved with. We don't like stepping on toes but we have an equal dislike for cheap fittings and poor workmanship.

  By getting straight to the point we don't mean to be rude, our prices don't account for small-talk or sales pitches, but yet we are still at the end of the phone when you need us!

Bathroom

Your whole family uses your bathroom on a daily basis. You might not invite overnight guests into your bedroom but you certainly wouldn't deprive them access to your bathroom.
This bathroom should be the standard to which your cloakrooms and ensuites aspire to.
Huge bath or walk in wet room, this is your preference and anything is possible. Choose your chinaware from our or any range and then we can provide options to compliment.

Cloakroom

Family, friends and general visitors will probably see this room.
When entertaining or relaxing you shouldn't need to take the stairs to find facilities of a high standard.
Half height tiles and a decorated finish to match the rest of your house provides a perfect balance whilst maintaining a spacious feel.
When you feel like a change a simple lick of paint can refresh without breaking the bank.

en suite

The en-suite is a private space, a tranquil area designed specifically for your relaxation.
It's an opportunity to create amazing, unique area. Previous projects have included everything from the standard his & hers sinks to jacuzzi baths, steam rooms and everything in-between.
This is your space and we take great pride in making it perfect.

We're not bragging but we've done this before

+ Bathrooms + Cloakrooms + En Suites

Leaving it will just cost more

Emergency Repairs & Maintenance

Is that discoloured patch on your ceiling part of the decor?

Stop tap emergency, “who you gonna call”?

Bathroom by LV can provide the solution. Stop ignoring the problem, lets not turn this leak into a flood

Find out more

The room you’ve always wanted

Refurbishment 

Is your bathroom looking tired and dated?

Jealous of the Jones’s smart bathroom?

Bathroom by LV can provide the solution. We can design and Modernise your bathroom using high quality materials and the latest technology.

Find out more

Utilise your available space.

Creation

Are you making the most of your space?

How chaotic are your morning bathroom routines?

Bathrooms by LV can provide the solution. We’re experienced in bathroom creation to reduce morning stress and start your day in the right way.

Find out more

About Us

Some Words

Having nearly two decades of experience gives the benefit of understanding and avoiding the pitfalls that can occur.

An "expert" is defined as "in excess of 10,000 hours", we believe an expert is understanding the needs and dreams and exceeding all expectations without compromise.

At present information about remedies changes regularly. Let's talk about divers medicaments you can get from Internet.

Each to their own: modern or traditional, ceramic or porcelain tiles, we'll conform and deliver to your exact specification. Unfortunately we can't resist speaking up if we think it's not quite right or could be better - honesty is our policy for better or worse.

Warrington based, with a great reputation and a network of trusted experts at our disposal.

We're proud of what we do, and as much as we want your business we'll only accept it if we are a perfect fit.

We don't have repeat customers - our bathrooms last.

Some feedback from our customers

Liam and his team previously designed and installed bathrooms at our current and previous homes and have always done a fantastic job, so when we needed a complete refurbishment of the upstairs rooms in our house, they were our obvious choice. We are delighted with the results, nothing is too much trouble and they even jumped in at short notice to help put things right after some issues with flooring bought on the high street. We would thoroughly recommend the services of bathrooms by LV and will be asking them back in a few months to do the finishing touches, radiators and joinery work. I genuinely wouldn't go anywhere else now I've found Liam and his team because I can totally rely on them doing a great job and taking the hassle out of finding all the different trades you need for home improvement projects Vicky & Michael Watson We had a complete bathroom refit carried out by Liam. I really couldn’t recommend him more highly, from start to finish his work is second to none. He really looked after the house putting dust sheets everywhere before he started work, and cleaning up every night before he left. We are so happy with our new bathroom, it’s amazing. Mariah and dickie Latchford

Actions speak louder than words

A select few of our recent projects

Get in touch

  • WeCare@LVBathrooms.co.uk

  • 01925 457024

The Wainwright family contacted me January 2020 for a 4 bathroom quote, unfortunately due to Covid the job had to be delayed. We have completely gutted every single bathroom, without leaving any old floorboards or walls in… We replaced everything so we know it is all brand new, start to finish! Every bathroom has a new waste from every appliance to the new stack outside. Every bathroom has - Porcelain tiles on the wall and floorFully sound proofed walls and floorsUnderfloor heatingNew electrics The whole job was supplied and designed by the team @Smithfields of Stockton Heath. We have gone above and beyond to pull these bathrooms out of the bag to finish on schedule.

posts.title: Hi-Tech bathrooms for the modern family
posts.content_raw:
Susan and Mark approached me earlier this year to achieve a refurbishment of four modern style, hi-tech bathrooms that would turn out to be one of the biggest projects I have taken on in my career.
With this project Mark my customer is some what of genius and whiz kid with technology so straight away up to date tech and spec was going to be involved in all of the bathrooms.
It was specified that all four of the hi-tech bathrooms required electrical outlets for speaker systems, illuminated mirrors, inset alcoves with lights and not forgetting charging points for all phones, toothbrushes and all other gadgets.
Toilets were going to be wired to fuse spurs as the toilets were SMART TOILETS with heated seats water jets and lights all built into the whole unit.
The main bathroom was to also have underfloor heating that was all to be controlled from the customers remote control.
First job was to remove the whole bathroom plus all of the floor substructure. It was apparent of quotation stage the floors would not support the tiles that the customer was supplying.
If the floor is not solid it will create flex and therefore the tiles will lift eventually.
All the tiles for this job were 1200mm x 500mm for the walls and 800mm x 500mm for the floors.
Not the average size tile but great to work with once we got going.
All the pipework was removed for each of the bathrooms including feeds to every appliance and all wastes to.
All wastes to each bathroom were upsized as we were adding to each bathroom more outlets than what was in originally and wanted to make sure the correct flow from each appliance was as adequate.
We added extra supports into to the bathrooms for the floors and boarded each bathroom with brand new 22mm floorboards.
The electricians then run in all the first fix from above to each new outlet ready for re-boarding the bathrooms.
Every single wall was boarded and prep ready for tiling or plastering in some parts (mainly just the apex of the ceilings which come down from the roof).
All of the wet decks, shower trays and bath were all the fitted into place so that the tiling could commence.
Tiling proceeded and went very well, we had to cut in all shapes and sizes of tiles to get these bathrooms to look that extra bit special.
We added 7 alcoves into the bathrooms all incorporating lights or illuminated mirrors with inset glass shelves.
The final finish was in sight after all the tiling was finished. We then used Grout 3000 which is an amazing grout to use and leaves hardly any smear afterwards therefore it polishes up perfectly.

All of the hi-tech bathrooms were second fixed with all the technology of a modern home being added into each individual bathroom.
The final finish was amazing and once again I have another family very happy that I still have been in contact with to sort more work with including the painting of every feature beam in the house.

As you will see Susan and Mark’s testimonial will be published on my website and social media.

posts.title: Traditional Bathrooms in fitting with the rest of your property
posts.content_raw:
With this bathroom Carl and Louise approached me and said they would like to go back to old school and were keen on traditional bathrooms.
So straight away I had the ideas or Victorian style taps and metro tiles. However there was a twist.
They wanted to incorporate black, anthracite and light grey colours  into the bathroom.
For me that is not a problem.
First job was to remove the whole bathroom and render behind so that I had a complete shell to work with. Then was completely stripped I cut out all of the hot and cold feeds to the whole bathroom in the loft so that I could start again.
I removed all waste pipes and with doing this I know with fresh drains nothing can go wrong on my side of the job and the flow will be 100% from each appliance in the bathroom.
All brand new pipework was run into the bathroom from above meaning there were no dead legs or original pipework. This for further down the line gives us the best result for second fixing everything.
All wastes were run into the property from the outside. Which completed all pipework in the bungalow.
All walls were then prepped and glued in preparation for the walls to be boarded and whilst we were there Carl asked could we put a brand new ceiling in which wasn’t a big job therefore we removed the other one quickly and efficiently and prepped also.
